content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN) and QQQ queries (numbered 111 through QQQ). For each valid iii, in the iii-th query, you are given KiK_iKi​ nodes x1,x2,…,xKix_1, x_2, \ldots, x_{K_i}x1​,x2​,…,xKi​​. Consider the smallest subtree which contains all of these nodes; you should find all centers of this subtree.

A node is called a center of a tree if it lies in the middle of at least one longest path in that tree. Note that there may be multiple longest paths (paths with the same maximum length) and for a longest path which contains an even number of nodes, there are two nodes lying in the middle of this path.

**Note:** A subtree here refers to a connected subgraph of the tree. Selecting a node does not mean all its descendants have to be selected.
constraints: the graph described on the input is a tree
1≤xi≤N1 \leq x_i \leq N1≤xi​≤N for each valid iii
for each query, x1,x2,…,xKix_1, x_2, \ldots, x_{K_i}x1​,x2​,…,xKi​​ are pairwise distinct
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105
the sum of KiK_iKi​ over all queries in all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
N−1N-1N−1 lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that there is an edge between nodes uuu and vvv in the tree.
QQQ more lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains an integer KiK_iKi​, followed by a space and KiK_iKi​ space-separated integers x1,x2,…,xKix_1, x_2, \ldots, x_{K_i}x1​,x2​,…,xKi​​.
**Note:** The input and output of this problem are large, so prefer using fast input/output methods.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing an integer CCC denoting the number of centers, followed by a space and CCC space-separated integers — the nodes which are centers of the given subtree, sorted in increasing order.
